title: TEN Framework: Voice AI That Can Actually Be Interrupted
summary: TEN Framework is an open-source framework for building real-time voice AI agents with streaming audio, interruption handling, VAD, turn detection, STT, LLM, TTS, avatars, telephony, and multimodal workflows. It addresses the pain point of natural conversation handling through graph-based extensions and real-time audio pipelines. The framework aims to solve production readiness issues where conventional voice agents fail under interruptions and overlapping speech.
writeup: This video covers TEN Framework, an open-source framework for building real-time voice AI agents that handle streaming audio and interruptions. It walks through the framework's components: voice activity detection, turn detection, STT, LLM, TTS, avatars, telephony, and multimodal workflows, all assembled through graph-based extensions and real-time audio pipelines. The focus is the conversation problem that conventional voice agents fail at, where overlapping speech and interruptions break production deployments. The takeaway is that TEN's pipeline and extension architecture targets natural turn-taking and interruption handling as a path to production readiness.
